evidence: The statement concerns sums of integers b_i = 2^{k_i}3^{l_i}, i.e. 3-smooth numbers.
The problem asks whether the set of such sums 'has density 0' (formalized via Set.HasDensity 0).
The docstring asks about subsets of the plane 'with no three on a line', i.e. avoiding collinear triples (NonTrilinear).
The docstring is about subsets containing 'no three-term arithmetic progression' (ThreeAPFree).
The sets 'which contain no three-term arithmetic progression' in the docstring are exactly Salem–Spencer (progression-free) sets.
The docstring asks for the maximum set A in {1,...,N} such that 'ab + 1 is never squarefree'.
Sawhney's asymptotic result concerns sets where 'ab + 1 is never squarefree for all a, b in A'.
The docstring asks whether the equation 'n choose k = a' has exactly t solutions for some a, for every t.
erdosproblems.com/849 tags this problem 'Singmaster's conjecture'; it asks the inverse question about multiplicities of binomial-coefficient values, not the boundedness statement itself.
f(n) is defined as the least minimum degree forcing a C_4 (cycleGraph 4) subgraph in an n-vertex graph.
erdosproblems.com/850 labels this the 'Erdős-Woods conjecture'; the decl states the k=2 instance (x, x+1, x+2 sharing prime factors), not the general exists-k conjecture.
The docstring asks for distinct x, y such that x,y (and x+1,y+1 and x+2,y+2) 'have the same prime factors'.
The variant named 'romanoff' states exactly Romanov's theorem: 'The set of integers of the form 2^k+p (where p is prime) has positive lower density'.
The problem asks whether 'the density of integers of the form 2^k+n ... is at least 1-epsilon' (Set.HasDensity).
The set TwoPowAddSet r is built from integers n with 'at most r prime divisors' (n.primeFactors.card <= r).
The docstring defines d_n = p_{n+1} - p_n (primeGap) and asks whether the least missing even gap r(x) tends to infinity.
Part (ii) asks whether r(x)/log x tends to infinity, where r(x) is the least even value missing among prime gaps d_n up to x.
